title: Montiel handball — World Cup Final 2022
match: Argentina vs France, World Cup Final 2022
question: Was this a handball by Montiel?
description: Extra time. France cross the ball into the box. Nicolás Otamendi misses his header, the ball drops toward goal, and Nahuel Molina's clearance hits Gonzalo Montiel's outstretched arm. The referee points to the spot. Kylian Mbappé scores the penalty to make it 3-3.
video_hint: Qatar 2022 final — 116th minute, France's equaliser from the spot
user_vote_prompt: Handball? You have 5 seconds — vote now.

referee_context.referee_position: 12m inside the penalty area, level with the six-yard box
referee_context.view_angle: Front-on to Montiel; saw arm contact but not whether the arm moved toward the ball
referee_context.pressure: World Cup final, extra time, Argentina leading 3-2 — one call from a shootout

pressure_context.view_quality: Front-on but chaotic — partially obstructed scramble
pressure_context.stakes: World Cup final, extra time, 3-2 lead

camera_context.broadcast_angle: Slow-motion replay from behind the goal, zoomed tight on the arm
camera_context.camera_limit: Replay makes the arm look further from the body than it appeared at full speed from the referee's angle
camera_context.missing_context: Whether Montiel's arm was already in a natural position when Molina's clearance deflected off it — the broadcast never showed the referee's exact sightline
camera_context.what_ref_saw: Ball struck an extended arm in the box during a chaotic scramble; immediate penalty signal

ground_truth_note: IFAB Law 12 — not every arm contact is handball; the arm must be in an unnaturally extended position or move toward the ball. Borderline on both counts.

title: Suárez on the line — Ghana 2010
match: Uruguay vs Ghana, World Cup Quarter-Final 2010
question: Was the red card and penalty the correct decision?
description: Last seconds of extra time. Dominic Ador's header beats the keeper and is heading into an empty net. Luis Suárez punches the ball off the line with both hands. Sent off. Asamoah Gyan hits the bar from the penalty. Uruguay win on penalties.
video_hint: Johannesburg 2010 — goal-line handball, missed penalty, shootout drama
user_vote_prompt: Correct call? Vote yes or no.
rule_citation: IFAB Law 12 — Denying an Obvious Goal

referee_context.referee_position: 8m from goal, unobstructed view of the goal line
referee_context.view_angle: Perfect sightline — saw the deliberate double-hand stop in real time
referee_context.pressure: Last action of extra time; Ghana one kick from becoming Africa's first semi-finalist

pressure_context.view_quality: Clear goal-line view — rare perfect sightline
pressure_context.stakes: Last seconds of extra time; continent's first semi-final at stake

camera_context.broadcast_angle: Multiple HD cameras including goal-line angle — one of the clearest replays in World Cup history
camera_context.camera_limit: Almost none for the foul itself — TV and referee saw the same deliberate act
camera_context.missing_context: The real debate is moral (gamesmanship vs cheating), not visual — cameras cannot show intent vs desperation
camera_context.what_ref_saw: Deliberate handball preventing a certain goal — mandatory red card and penalty

ground_truth_note: IFAB Law 12 — handball denying an obvious goal-scoring opportunity: red card and penalty. The call is correct; the punishment debate is emotional, not legal.

title: Sterling penalty — England vs Denmark
question: Was this a penalty on Raheem Sterling?
description: Extra time at Wembley. Raheem Sterling drives into the box. Joakim Maehle sticks out a leg. Sterling goes down. The referee pauses — then points to the spot. Harry Kane's penalty is saved but he scores the rebound.
video_hint: Euro 2020 semi — extra time, Wembley, laser pointer on the keeper

referee_context.referee_position: 18m away, trailing the play at an angle from the left
referee_context.view_angle: Saw Sterling go down and Maehle's leg extend — could not clearly judge force or whether contact caused the fall
referee_context.pressure: Extra time semi-final at home; no VAR review of the foul decision itself

pressure_context.view_quality: Trailing angle — force of contact unclear
pressure_context.stakes: Euro semi-final extra time; laser pointer on keeper

camera_context.broadcast_angle: Slow-motion from behind the goal shows minimal knee-to-knee contact
camera_context.camera_limit: Replay magnifies contact that looked like a dive at full speed; reverse angle never shown live
camera_context.missing_context: A laser pointer was visible on the Danish keeper from the crowd — the referee and cameras were dealing with multiple distractions simultaneously
camera_context.what_ref_saw: Sterling past his man, leg contact, attacker on the ground in the box

ground_truth_note: IFAB Law 12 — a penalty requires a careless, reckless, or excessively forceful challenge. Many referees would let this go; others call it on contact alone.

title: Lautaro offside — Argentina vs Saudi Arabia
match: Argentina vs Saudi Arabia, World Cup Group Stage 2022
question: Was Lautaro Martínez offside before scoring?
description: Argentina 1-0 up and dominating. Lautaro Martínez runs through and finishes past the keeper. Flag stays down. Then VAR intervenes — the goal is chalked off for offside by a margin so tight the stadium falls silent. It happens again minutes later.
video_hint: Qatar 2022 — Argentina's shock loss begins with two VAR offside calls

referee_context.referee_position: 40m from goal, following play — not in line with the defensive line
referee_context.view_angle: Could not judge marginal offside; deferred entirely to VAR
referee_context.pressure: Opening group game; Argentina expected to win easily — VAR delay kills momentum twice

pressure_context.view_quality: VAR-only — marginal pixel lines invisible live
pressure_context.stakes: Shock upset brewing; Argentina's campaign in balance

camera_context.broadcast_angle: Semi-automated offside lines drawn on broadcast replays — not available to anyone in the stadium live
camera_context.camera_limit: TV draws a pixel-thin line on a shoulder or armpit; fans cannot see this in real time from any seat or standard camera
camera_context.missing_context: Which body part was measured (shoulder vs foot) and the exact frame when the pass was played — both changed between broadcast angles
camera_context.what_ref_saw: Flag down initially; after VAR check, told to disallow for offside by the narrowest margin

ground_truth_note: IFAB Law 11 — offside is judged on any part of the body except hands/arms. Semi-automated VAR makes calls possible that no human eye could make in real time.

title: Llorente handball — Tottenham vs Ajax
match: Ajax vs Tottenham, Champions League Semi-Final 2019 (2nd Leg)
question: Did Llorente handball before Lucas Moura's winner?
description: Ajax lead 3-2 on aggregate. A cross drops to Fernando Llorente. The ball hits his arm and drops to Lucas Moura, who volleys in the winner on the turn. Ajax players scream for handball. The referee allows the goal. Tottenham reach the final.
video_hint: Amsterdam 2019 — stoppage time, Lucas Moura hat-trick, handball appeal ignored

referee_context.referee_position: 25m away, side-on to Llorente as the cross arrived
referee_context.view_angle: Saw the ball come off Llorente's upper body but at speed could not distinguish arm from chest
referee_context.pressure: Stoppage time in a Champions League semi-final; no VAR in Europe that season

pressure_context.crowd: 52,000 at Johan Cruyff Arena — Ajax fans silenced
pressure_context.view_quality: Side-on — arm vs chest indistinguishable at speed
pressure_context.stakes: Champions League semi stoppage time; no VAR to check

camera_context.broadcast_angle: Replay from behind the goal shows the ball clearly striking Llorente's arm before falling to Moura
camera_context.camera_limit: The referee had no replay — only fans at home saw the arm contact on the first replay cycle
camera_context.missing_context: Whether the arm was in a natural position when the cross arrived; the side-on live angle made chest and arm look identical
camera_context.what_ref_saw: Ball deflected off Llorente's upper body toward Moura — played on instantly with no hesitation

ground_truth_note: IFAB Law 12 — if the ball touches the arm and creates a goal-scoring opportunity, it should be handball. Replays suggest the arm was involved; the referee had no technology to check.
